timing_caveat: directional only: shared mode also has more host submits

blockers.ring_routing: HCQProgram.__call__ (hcq.py:374) hardcodes hw_compute_queue_t()==ring0; live decode with AMD_COMPUTE_RINGS=2 leaves dev.compute_queues=={0}. Need per-stream ring selection.
blockers.global_timeline: device has ONE timeline_signal; every op waits/signals it (hcq.py:307/374). Two streams sharing it serialize even on two rings. Need independent per-stream timelines.
blockers.model_state: one cache_kv per model; two concurrent streams need two state/cache sets.

verdict: BLOCKED by dispatch path (NOT hardware): a real decode with AMD_COMPUTE_RINGS=2 uses rings [0] -- ring 1 is never touched (no per-stream ring-selection API). And the shared global timeline serializes dispatch (directionally: HCQProgram-style 1.464x slower than independent per-stream signals, even on two rings). Missing APIs: (1) per-stream/per-graph ring selection, (2) independent per-stream timelines.
